---
title: "Get Ready for the Course"
description: "Accounts to open and tools to install before Day 1 — takes about 30 minutes"
toc: false
---

<span class="sl" style="color:#A78BFA">// PRE-COURSE SETUP &nbsp;·&nbsp; 课前准备</span>

```{=html}
<div style="display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; gap:2rem; align-items:flex-start; margin:2rem 0 2.5rem;">

  <!-- Team photo -->
  <div style="flex:1; min-width:260px; max-width:380px;">
    <img src="images/team.jpg" alt="The Maker-X instructors" style="width:100%; border-radius:10px; opacity:0.92;">
  </div>

  <!-- Encouraging text -->
  <div style="flex:1; min-width:240px;">
    <p style="font-family:'Exo 2',sans-serif; font-size:1.15rem; font-weight:700; color:#fff; margin:0 0 .8rem;">
      Don't panic. We're all figuring this out.
    </p>
    <p style="font-family:'Barlow',sans-serif; font-size:0.92rem; color:#9AACBF; line-height:1.7; margin:0 0 .9rem;">
      Some of these tools didn't exist two years ago. One or two will probably change before the course starts.
      The documentation will have gaps. The AI will occasionally hallucinate something spectacular.
      This is not a sign you're doing it wrong — it's a sign you're at the frontier.
    </p>
    <p style="font-family:'Barlow',sans-serif; font-size:0.92rem; color:#9AACBF; line-height:1.7; margin:0 0 .9rem;">
      Your instructors are learning too — we just have a head start of a few months.
      The best thing you can do is show up curious, set up the tools below, and be ready to break things on Day 1.
    </p>
    <p style="font-family:'Barlow',sans-serif; font-size:0.88rem; color:rgba(255,255,255,0.35); font-style:italic;">
      Total setup time: ~30 minutes. Most of it is waiting for downloads.
    </p>
  </div>
</div>

<p style="font-family:'Audiowide',sans-serif; font-size:1.25rem; color:#FCD34D; text-align:center; margin:0 0 2.5rem; letter-spacing:.03em;">
  Get ready to break things on Day 1.
</p>

<!-- Setup checklist -->
<style>
.setup-item {
  display: flex;
  gap: 1.4rem;
  align-items: flex-start;
  padding: 1.4rem;
  border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.07);
  border-radius: 10px;
  margin-bottom: 1rem;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.025);
  transition: border-color .2s;
}
.setup-item:hover { border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.14); }
.setup-num {
  font-family: 'Audiowide', sans-serif;
  font-size: 1.5rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  min-width: 2.2rem;
  line-height: 1;
  margin-top: .15rem;
}
.setup-body { flex: 1; }
.setup-title {
  font-family: 'Exo 2', sans-serif;
  font-size: 1rem;
  font-weight: 800;
  color: #fff;
  margin: 0 0 .3rem;
  letter-spacing: .02em;
}
.setup-desc {
  font-family: 'Barlow', sans-serif;
  font-size: 0.86rem;
  color: #9AACBF;
  line-height: 1.65;
  margin: 0 0 .7rem;
}
.setup-steps {
  font-family: 'Barlow', sans-serif;
  font-size: 0.84rem;
  color: #CBD5E1;
  line-height: 1.8;
  margin: 0 0 .7rem;
  padding-left: 1rem;
}
.setup-steps li { margin-bottom: .1rem; }
.setup-link {
  display: inline-block;
  font-family: 'Exo 2', sans-serif;
  font-size: 0.68rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  letter-spacing: .1em;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  text-decoration: none;
  color: rgba(255,255,255,0.55);
  border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.15);
  padding: 3px 12px;
  border-radius: 20px;
  margin: 2px 4px 2px 0;
  transition: border-color .2s, color .2s;
}
.setup-link:hover { color: #fff; border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.4); }
.setup-note {
  font-family: 'Barlow', sans-serif;
  font-size: 0.78rem;
  color: rgba(255,255,255,0.3);
  font-style: italic;
  margin-top: .4rem;
}
</style>

<div class="setup-item">
  <div class="setup-num" style="color:#FCD34D">01</div>
  <div class="setup-body">
    <div class="setup-title">GitHub + Copilot</div>
    <div class="setup-desc">
      GitHub is where your team's code lives — it's a deliverable. Copilot is GitHub's AI pair programmer,
      built into VS Code. It completes code, explains unfamiliar functions, and writes boilerplate so you can focus on the interesting parts.
    </div>
    <ol class="setup-steps">
      <li>Create a free GitHub account at <strong>github.com</strong></li>
      <li>Apply for the GitHub Education Pack at <strong>education.github.com/pack</strong> — use your <strong>@shanghaitech.edu.cn</strong> email. Approval takes 1–3 days and gives you Copilot for free.</li>
      <li>Install GitHub Copilot as a VS Code extension (step 5 — set up VS Code first if needed)</li>
    </ol>
    <a class="setup-link" href="https://github.com" target="_blank">→ github.com</a>
    <a class="setup-link" href="https://education.github.com/pack" target="_blank">→ Education Pack</a>
    <div class="setup-note">Apply for the Education Pack as early as possible — approval is not instant.</div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="setup-item">
  <div class="setup-num" style="color:#60A5FA">02</div>
  <div class="setup-body">
    <div class="setup-title">OpenRouter</div>
    <div class="setup-desc">
      OpenRouter is a single API gateway to 300+ AI models — Qwen, DeepSeek, Claude, and others.
      Your ESP32 will call OpenRouter to get your robot's AI responses.
      You use one API key, one endpoint, and can swap models with a one-line change.
    </div>
    <ol class="setup-steps">
      <li>Create a free account at <strong>openrouter.ai</strong></li>
      <li>Go to <strong>Settings → API Keys</strong> and create a key — save it somewhere safe</li>
      <li>Add $5–10 of credit under <strong>Settings → Credits</strong> — this will last the entire course</li>
    </ol>
    <a class="setup-link" href="https://openrouter.ai" target="_blank">→ openrouter.ai</a>
    <a class="setup-link" href="https://openrouter.ai/models" target="_blank">→ Browse models</a>
    <div class="setup-note">$5 is genuinely enough. Each LLM call costs fractions of a cent at the models we use.</div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="setup-item">
  <div class="setup-num" style="color:#FB923C">03</div>
  <div class="setup-body">
    <div class="setup-title">Blender</div>
    <div class="setup-desc">
      Blender is the 3D modeling tool you'll use to design your robot's enclosure — the physical body that makes it a <em>product</em>, not just a circuit on a table.
      It's free, open-source, and runs on any computer.
      You don't need to know it yet — we'll learn the basics together.
    </div>
    <ol class="setup-steps">
      <li>Download and install Blender from <strong>blender.org/download</strong> — get the latest stable release</li>
      <li>Optional head start: watch the first 2 episodes of the <a href="https://www.blenderguru.com/posts/blender-donut-v5-tutorial" target="_blank" style="color:#FB923C;">Blender Guru donut tutorial</a> — it covers the core interface in ~30 minutes</li>
    </ol>
    <a class="setup-link" href="https://www.blender.org/download/" target="_blank">→ blender.org/download</a>
    <div class="setup-note">Free forever. No account needed.</div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="setup-item">
  <div class="setup-num" style="color:#A78BFA">04</div>
  <div class="setup-body">
    <div class="setup-title">Claude Pro</div>
    <div class="setup-desc">
      Claude Pro gives you access to <strong>Claude Code</strong> — Anthropic's agentic AI coding tool that runs in your terminal.
      It understands your entire codebase, writes firmware, explains ESP-IDF errors, and can refactor across multiple files at once.
      It's the fastest way to go from "I have no idea what this error means" to "it's working."
    </div>
    <ol class="setup-steps">
      <li>Sign up at <strong>claude.ai</strong> — use any email</li>
      <li>Subscribe to <strong>Claude Pro</strong> ($20/month) — check if your institution has a discount or arrangement</li>
      <li>Install Claude Code in your terminal: <code>npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code</code></li>
    </ol>
    <a class="setup-link" href="https://claude.ai" target="_blank">→ claude.ai</a>
    <a class="setup-link" href="https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/overview" target="_blank">→ Claude Code docs</a>
    <div class="setup-note">Requires Node.js. If you don't have it, install from nodejs.org first.</div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="setup-item">
  <div class="setup-num" style="color:#22D3EE">05</div>
  <div class="setup-body">
    <div class="setup-title">VS Code + PlatformIO + ESP-IDF</div>
    <div class="setup-desc">
      VS Code is the editor. PlatformIO is the extension that handles ESP32 board config, library management, and flashing.
      ESP-IDF is Espressif's official development framework that PlatformIO uses under the hood.
      Together they turn your laptop into a proper embedded development workstation.
    </div>
    <ol class="setup-steps">
      <li>Download and install VS Code from <strong>code.visualstudio.com</strong></li>
      <li>Open VS Code → Extensions (⌘⇧X) → search <strong>PlatformIO IDE</strong> → Install</li>
      <li>PlatformIO will install ESP-IDF automatically when you first open an ESP32 project</li>
      <li>While you're in Extensions, also install <strong>GitHub Copilot</strong> (from step 1)</li>
    </ol>
    <a class="setup-link" href="https://code.visualstudio.com" target="_blank">→ VS Code</a>
    <a class="setup-link" href="https://platformio.org/install/ide?install=vscode" target="_blank">→ PlatformIO for VS Code</a>
    <a class="setup-link" href="https://docs.espressif.com/projects/esp-idf/en/stable/esp32/get-started/index.html" target="_blank">→ ESP-IDF docs</a>
    <div class="setup-note">All free. The PlatformIO first-time install downloads ~500 MB of toolchains — do it on Wi-Fi.</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

---

::: {.callout-tip title="Already done? Try a hello world."}
Flash the built-in LED on your ESP32 before class. If you can get a blink running, you're ahead of the game.

```cpp
// paste this into a PlatformIO project's src/main.cpp
#include <Arduino.h>
void setup() { pinMode(2, OUTPUT); }
void loop()  { digitalWrite(2, HIGH); delay(500);
               digitalWrite(2, LOW);  delay(500); }
```
:::
